example: Even when things are difficult, you should never **give up** on your dreams.
I know the exam is hard, but **you can do it** if you study hard.
To achieve success, you must first **believe in yourself** and your abilities.
When giving a presentation, you should **be confident** and speak clearly.
You got an A on the test! **Good job**!
**Well done** on completing the project ahead of schedule.
The teacher praised the student for her **excellent work** on the science project.
Your English grades show **great improvement** this semester.
**You made progress** in math since last month.
**Keep up the good work** and you will achieve your goals.
**I'm proud of you** for standing up for what you believe in.
With that solution, it's clear that **you are smart** and creative.
**You are clever** to figure out the answer so quickly.
You have a gift for music, you **are talented** in art.
You need to **work harder** to improve your grades.
Please **study more** if you want to pass the exam.
You should **read more books** to expand your vocabulary.
**Practice every day** and you will get better.
**Don't be lazy** with your studies.
Please **be active** in class discussions.
Don't be afraid to **be brave** and try new things.
**Don't be shy** to share your ideas.
Please **speak up** so everyone can hear.
Please **join the discussion** and share your thoughts.
Feel free to **share your opinion** with the class.
Learn to **express yourself** clearly in English.
Try to **be creative** with your writing.
**Use your imagination** to write a story.
**Think outside** the box for innovative solutions.
We need to **solve problems** together.
Work in teams to **find solutions** to challenges.
Sometimes you need to **work independently** on projects.
Learn to **cooperate with others** in group work.
To succeed, you must **be a team player**.
Always **respect others**' opinions and feelings.
**Be polite** to everyone you meet.
Always **help others** when they need assistance.
**Support your classmates** when they face challenges.
We should **encourage each other** to do our best.
Let's **learn together** and improve.
We will **grow together** this year.
We can **succeed together** as a class.
**Today's homework is** exercises 1-5 on page 20.
**For homework**, please finish the writing task.
**Do exercise** 6 on page 21 at home.
You can find the answers **on page** 45.
**Write a short passage** about your family.
**Make a poster** about your favourite sport.
Please **prepare for** the presentation next week.
**Get ready for** tomorrow's English class.
